text.title: reflexion-learning Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for reflexion-learning as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Loading the full patterns.md without keyword filtering before a task.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A mature project's patterns.md can reach 15-20k tokens. Full loads exceed the practical context budget before the task description is even added.

failure-mode.description: Agents assign initial confidence of 0.9 to new patterns because the first use succeeded.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: One success does not validate cross-context applicability. New patterns start at 0.5 regardless of first-use outcome.

failure-mode.description: Confidence scores over 90 days old treated as current without verification.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Framework upgrades, architecture changes, and library version bumps can invalidate high-confidence patterns silently. Treat scores older than 90 days as unreliable until re-validated.

failure-mode.description: Multiple agents writing to patterns.md concurrently.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Concurrent writes produce last-write-wins corruption of confidence scores. In multi-agent workflows, serialize memory writes through a single coordinator agent.
